title: andropogon lateralis var. incanus
intro: Andropogon lateralis var. incanus, also known as wild sugarcane or Indian staff, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive discomfort, and promoting skin healing. In herbal preparations, Andropogon lateralis var. incanus can be made into teas, infusions, or used in poultices to harness its medicinal properties.
